steps.title: Follow the passage structure
steps.content: The text begins by introducing Elena L. Cardelús as a researcher who studies canopy soil. It then explains what canopy soil is, why it matters in rainforests, and why Cardelús studies it in large choibá trees.

steps.title: Match the research focus
steps.content: The matching choice captures that structure: first the researcher, then information about her research. Choice A invents a disagreement, Choice B invents a researcher who disagrees with an idea, and Choice C invents a size comparison between trees. The answer is D.
